questions.question: The synchronous reactance of an alternator is due to

answers.b: reactance because of armature reaction in the machine
answers.d: both leakage flux as well as armature reaction

explanations.a: Incorrect. Leakage flux alone does not account for synchronous reactance; armature reaction also plays a role.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Armature reaction is a component, but synchronous reactance includes both leakage reactance and armature reaction reactance.
explanations.c: Incorrect. This is a circular definition.
explanations.d: Correct. Synchronous reactance Xs = Xl (leakage reactance) + Xa (armature reaction reactance).

questions.question: The saturated synchronous reactance of an alternator is ---------- its unsaturated synchronous reactance

explanations.a: Incorrect. Saturation reduces the effective reactance.
explanations.b: Correct. Due to magnetic saturation, the reactance decreases compared to unsaturated condition.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Saturation causes non-linearity, so they are not equal.

questions.question: Unbalanced three phase stator currents cause

answers.c: Double frequency currents in the rotor

explanations.a: Partially correct but incomplete. Vibrations occur due to negative sequence fields.
explanations.b: Partially correct but incomplete. Rotor heating is caused by induced currents.
explanations.c: Partially correct but incomplete. Double frequency currents are induced in the rotor.
explanations.d: Correct. All three effects (vibrations, rotor heating, and double frequency currents) occur due to unbalanced currents.

questions.question: The short circuit characteristic of alternator is

explanations.a: Correct. Due to negligible armature reaction and operation in linear region of magnetization, SCC is a straight line.
explanations.b: Incorrect. SCC is linear because the machine operates in unsaturated region during short circuit.
explanations.c: Incorrect. It is always linear for practical purposes.

questions.question: Mention the fault in which only positive sequence reactance is present

explanations.a: Incorrect. L-L fault involves positive and negative sequences only.
explanations.b: Incorrect. L-L-G fault involves all three sequences.
explanations.c: Correct. A balanced three-phase fault has only positive sequence components.
explanations.d: Incorrect. L-G fault involves positive, negative, and zero sequences.
